title: Latchline Commit Button
prompt:
Create a production-ready Latchline Commit Button using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original button represents a deliberate publish action: a small lemon latch travels along a dark baseline after the user activates the control, then the label changes from Commit draft to Committed. Place it in a light Bento stage with white and #F4F5F6 surfaces, 22px rounded corners, a #ECECEE border, a very soft shadow, #111111 primary text, and #4A4A4F support copy. Use lemon as the only bright accent; the button itself must not use a gradient.

Use one semantic button with a 44px minimum target, an explicit label, aria-pressed, and a polite live status. Click, Enter, and Space must toggle the committed state. On commit, move the latch with a spring around stiffness 380 and damping 26, compress the button to 0.98 for 90ms, and fade the status copy over 150ms ease-out. A second activation should safely return it to the draft state. Keep all state local, use transform and opacity instead of layout animation, and avoid timers, random values, storage, network requests, and remote assets.

The component must remain clear inside a compact card at 320px and grow naturally in a larger detail preview without fixed positioning. Preserve visible 3px keyboard focus, logical tab order, AA contrast, and text plus icon cues rather than color alone. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the latch to its final location and limiting the change to a short opacity fade. Return complete paste-ready client code plus a runnable pure CSS approximation and suggested HTML.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [done,setDone]=React.useState(false);return
RELEASE LATCH

{done?'Draft is committed.':'Draft is ready for review.'}

}return ;})()
